attacking_style: Opportunistic breakaway hunter who targets queen stages from long-range elite breaks (29-rider groups). Capable of bridging significant gaps (40+ seconds) on climbs then competing for the win, but lacks tactical sprint experience against veterans.
team_tactics: Deployed primarily as climbing domestique with freedom to hunt stages from breakaways when team GC ambitions are settled. Pace-setting duties in third week of Grand Tours, suggesting trust in stamina over explosive support.
terrain_preference: Hardest summit finishes suit him best, winning queen stage from breakaway. Unexpectedly strong time trialist for climber profile (22-23 seconds down from winners, top-10 checkpoints). Long climbs favor his diesel engine over punchy gradients.
vulnerability: Catastrophically short recovery window after hard efforts—lost 5 minutes within 2 days of winning queen stage. Inexperienced in tactical sprints when breakaways succeed. Prone to race-ending crashes in critical moments (Vuelta 2023).
key_patterns: Wins/places high from large breakaways on hardest mountain stages, never from reduced peloton sprints.
Strong unexpected TT performances across multiple Grand Tours, 20-25 seconds from winners despite climber classification.
Severe recovery deficit after maximum efforts: 5-minute losses within 48 hours of stage wins.
Improves form deep into three-week races, moving into top-10 GC while doing domestique work.
Hunts bonus seconds aggressively when in GC contention, gaining positions by single-second margins.
Lacks finishing composure against experienced riders in small-group sprints despite similar form levels.
form_trajectory: neutral: Showed strong late-Giro form (top-10 with pacing duties) in 2023, but Vuelta ended prematurely in crash before demonstrating peak. Mixed signals from most recent complete race block.
